title: Simple 1x6 Storage Shelf
summary: A small wall-mounted shelf with two side brackets and a flat metal rod running underneath as a towel or utensil bar, cut entirely from one 1x8 board.
description:
The whole shelf comes from a single 1x8 board: two side brackets and the shelf itself, nailed together into a small bracket shelf. **A 3/4" flat metal bar spans the underside of the two brackets**, cut to length with a jigsaw fitted with a metal-cutting blade — this bar is what turns a plain shelf into a usable hanging rail for towels, mugs, or utensils.

Mount directly into wall studs behind the drywall using a stud finder — screw straight through the back of the shelf into the stud rather than relying on anchors, since anything hung from the metal bar adds real leverage on the mounting screws.

This is a fast, minimal-parts build — sand and finish the wood before final assembly if a painted or stained look is wanted, since the metal bar makes it awkward to finish the wood cleanly afterward.

tools.note: Fitted with a metal-cutting blade, for cutting the flat metal bar.

materials.name: Wood screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"
Wall mounting screws (sized to your wall type)
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Build the shelf bracket
steps.body: Nail the side brackets to the shelf and back boards to form the shelf assembly.

steps.title: Add the metal bar
steps.body: Cut the metal bar to length with a jigsaw and metal-cutting blade, then mount it underneath the two side brackets.
materials: Wood screws, 2", or pocket hole screws, 1-1/4"

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand and finish the wood before mounting.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Mount to the wall
steps.body: Locate wall studs with a stud finder and screw through the back of the shelf directly into the studs.
materials: Wall mounting screws (sized to your wall type)

unresolvedImages.alt: Simple wall shelf with metal bar underneath
